stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says ceasefire still ‘in effect’ as Iran, US clash
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating the ceasefire by targeting Iranian vessels and carrying out strikes on coastal areas.

stories.headline: Trump says US-Iran ceasefire still in place after exchange of fire in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating the truce, alleging it targeted an oil tanker and carried out attacks on coastal areas.

stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire still in effect after US and Iran clash
stories.summary: UAE intercepts Iranian drones and missiles as tensions escalate

stories.headline: US Strikes Iran as Trump Demands Quick Signing of Deal
stories.summary: The US strikes Iranian targets following what it said were attacks on Navy destroyers, as President Donald Trump warned of "violent" escalation unless a deal is signed quickly. It leaves markets in "wait and see" mode as oil nears $100 a barrel. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iranian Targets; Iran Says It Returned Fire
stories.summary: The exchange was the latest twist in a week of mixed signals in the region and tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump searches for an off-ramp in the war that he started.

stories.headline: U.S. strikes Iran in response to attacks on warships, military says
stories.summary: The burst of violence cast fresh doubt on efforts by Washington and Tehran to reach a negotiated settlement that would end hostilities.

stories.headline: Oil prices jump as US, Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Brent crude rises amid clashes in critical waterway.

stories.headline: Why Oil May Hit $90 in Truce or $150 in Escalation
stories.summary: There are three scenarios for oil prices based on how long the Iran war lasts, according to Jacky Tang, emerging markets chief investment officer at Deutsche Bank's private banking arm. (Updates clip from around 2:10 to include the third base case scenario.) (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Gundlach Takes Longshot Bet in Case of US Debt Restructuring
stories.summary: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach is repositioning some of his funds for the extreme scenario that the US government could choose to restructure its debt in response to a potential future recession.

stories.headline: Trump Rejects Need for Curbs on Oil Exports After Trade Surge
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said there’s no need for curbs on US oil or jet fuel exports to address global shortages sparked by the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Tennessee approves map dismantling majority-Black district
stories.summary: Tennessee has approved a new congressional map that breaks apart a majority-Black district centred on Memphis.

stories.headline: Anthropic weighs deal for near $1tn valuation as revenue surges
stories.summary: Start-up behind Claude tool is fielding inbound investment offers that could lead to it surpassing rival OpenAI in value

stories.headline: Labour suffers heavy early local election losses as Reform surges
stories.summary: More than 5,000 council seats are up for grabs in England, as parties also vie for control of the Scottish parliament and Welsh Senedd

stories.headline: ChinaAMC to Accelerate Tokenization Growth
stories.summary: ChinaAMC CEO Yimei Li says technology upgrades and semiconductors are driving market momentum in China, supporting strong performance in the firm’s ETF products. She also outlines plans for tokenization growth in an exclusive interview on Bloomberg’s ETF IQ Asia. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indonesia Foreign Reserves Fall Further to Two-Year Low in April
stories.summary: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell further in April to the lowest in nearly two years as the central bank stepped up intervention to defend the rupiah.

stories.headline: Markets Debate If Japan Sold Treasuries When Intervening in Yen
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve’s custody holdings of Treasuries fell for the first time in a month at a time Japan was likely intervening to support its currency, with markets debating whether the nation offloaded US securities to fund its yen purchases.

stories.headline: Australia’s Battery Boom Starts to Crowd Lucrative Power Trade
stories.summary: Australia’s big-battery build-out is starting to crowd the lucrative trade that made the assets attractive: buying up power when prices are low and selling it back when they spike, capturing the difference.

stories.headline: Adani Energy Stocks Emerge as Proxy for India’s AI Trade
stories.summary: Group’s push into green-powered data centers is driving gains in its energy units.

stories.headline: Tencent, Alibaba Face Slowing Growth as AI Costs Mount
stories.summary: Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. earnings will be shaped by rising artificial intelligence investment costs and growing competition in China’s AI sector, following DeepSeek’s high-profile V4 model launch.

stories.headline: Billionaire Piramal’s Shadow Bank Plans $1 Billion Foreign Debt
stories.summary: Billionaire Ajay Piramal’s Indian shadow lender is looking to raise as much as $1 billion in foreign currency loans this year to meet strong demand for retail credit in the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

stories.headline: AI Trade Turns Adani’s Energy Stocks Into India Proxy Play
stories.summary: Adani Group’s push into green-powered data centers is driving gains in its energy units, which have become one of the few artificial intelligence trades in India.

stories.headline: Australia Moves to Toughen Pension Funds’ Performance Scorecard
stories.summary: The Australian government is moving to tighten an annual performance test for the nation’s A$4.5 trillion ($3.2 trillion) pension industry as concerns grow the current model may be steering investment away from some sectors.

stories.headline: Australia news live: ABC reporters denied entry to One Nation press conference in Albury
stories.summary: Guardian Australia understands some journalists from The Australian and Daily Telegraph newspapers were allowed in. stories.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange fire in Strait of Hormuz, Trump says ceasefire holds
stories.summary: The US military said it struck Iranian targets in self-defence but that it does not seek escalation. Iran said it launched missiles at "enemy" forces in the waterway after a claimed US attack on an oil tanker, and reported shooting down several drones.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU until 4 July to implement trade deal or face 'much higher' tariffs
stories.summary: After speaking with Ursula von der Leyen on Thursday evening, Donald Trump delayed his threat of "much higher" tariffs on the European Union until 4 July. Von der Leyen hailed "good progress" towards implementation.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.headline: Palestinian boy mourns father killed in Israeli strike on security post
stories.summary: A Palestinian boy mourns his father, one of three people killed in an Israeli strike on a security post in Gaza.

stories.headline: Trump sets July 4 deadline for EU tariff hike decision
stories.summary: Trump claimed he had a 'great call' European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen about the looming tariffs.

stories.headline: Trump’s 10% global tariff ruled illegal by US court
stories.summary: Court of International Trade says levies applied under Section 122 of trade act are ‘unauthorized by law’

stories.summary: Trump also issues new deadline for EU to implement trade deal terms before raising tariffs to ‘much higher levels’The US trade court on Thursday ruled against Donald Trump’s latest 10% global tariffs, finding across-the-board tariffs were not justified under a 1970s trade law.The US court of international trade ruled in favor of small businesses that challenged the tariffs, which took effect on 24 February. The ruling was 2-1, with one judge sayi

stories.headline: US trade court rules against Trump’s 10 percent global tariffs
stories.summary: The court ruled in favour of small businesses that challenged the tariffs that took place in February.

stories.headline: Can China help end the Iran war?
stories.summary: Chinese and Iranian foreign ministers meet while a tense ceasefire holds.

stories.headline: ‘We will not be bullied’: MEPs dig in over delayed US trade deal
stories.summary: Nine months after the EU and US struck a controversial trade deal, negotiations remain deadlocked as MEPs resist pressure to fast-track approval without safeguards against future American threats.

stories.headline: Europe needs to be more radical on the energy crisis. But how? EU Climate Commissioner explains
stories.summary: The only way out of energy crises fueled by the wars in Iran and Ukraine is homegrown energy, EU Climate Commissioner Wopke Hoekstra told Euronews. "We owe it to our citizens and our companies."

stories.headline: Middle East war will slow down economic growth in Africa as cost of living crisis soars, IMF warns
stories.summary: According to the IMF's report, regional growth is expected to decline by 0.3 percentage points, and some 20 million people in Africa now face the risk of food insecurity as the Iran war raises energy and food prices.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
stories.summary: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

stories.headline: Valverde cut head when he 'accidentally hit table' in Tchouameni row
stories.summary: Federico Valverde went to hospital after a dressing room row with Real Madrid team-mate Aurelien Tchouameni which left him with a head injury after he "accidentally hit a table".

stories.headline: Ukraine war 'led me to surrogacy' to earn money but a new law could end those plans
stories.summary: Ukraine is considering a law that would effectively ban foreigners from accessing surrogacy in the country.

stories.headline: Anti-war protests rock Japan as PM pushes for stronger defence
stories.summary: Japan's largest anti-war protests in decades highlight a population divided over changes to its pacifist constitution.

stories.headline: Thousands of North Koreans fought for Russia. A memorial hints at the death toll
stories.summary: An estimated 11,000 North Korean soldiers were sent to fight in Russia's war against Ukraine.

stories.headline: Gold Rises as Buying Interest Counters Concern Over Hormuz Clash
stories.summary: Gold rose as signs of buying interest emerged in the wake of strong purchases by China’s central bank, even as fresh clashes in the Middle East threatened to fracture a fragile ceasefire.

stories.headline: Charlotte, N.C. Mayor Vi Lyles Says She Will Resign in May
stories.summary: Mayor Vi Lyles did not fully explain why she would leave office early, saying only that she wanted to spend more time with family.

stories.headline: Trump pledged to free Jimmy Lai. Now U.S. lawmakers want action at China summit.
stories.summary: The president previously committed to helping the 78-year-old Lai, a symbol of Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement who has been imprisoned since 2020.

stories.headline: Real Madrid’s Valverde hospitalised with head injury after Tchouameni fight
stories.summary: Real's turbulent season takes another twist on eve of El Clasico with Federico Valverde hurt in dressing room clash.

stories.headline: Tennessee approves new congressional map in latest redistricting flurry
stories.summary: Republicans pass new map ahead of midterms after US Supreme Court weakened Voting Rights Act restrictions.

stories.headline: Rwandan singer dies as he was being released from prison
stories.summary: The former university lecturer was a vocal critic of the government and ruling Rwandan Patriotic Front party.

stories.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers
stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.headline: 'I wouldn't pay it' - Trump on USA ticket price
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he "wouldn't pay it either" when asked about the potential ticket price to watch his country play Paraguay in their 2026 World Cup opener.

stories.headline: Disturbances and 127 arrests mar Paris party after PSG Champions League victory
stories.summary: Fans took to the streets to celebrate the Paris team's qualification for the final, but celebrations were marred by disturbances.

stories.headline: DR Congo president hints at extending his term and delaying polls
stories.summary: "If the people want me to have a third term, I will accept," Tshisekedi says, with him due to step down in 2028.

stories.headline: Top BJP leader's aide shot dead in violence after Indian state election
stories.summary: Chandranath Rath was personal assistant to Suvendu Adhikari, front runner to become West Bengal's new chief minister.

stories.headline: Crowds cheer China's new snooker star on return from championship win
stories.summary: Wu Yize, 22, receives a rock star's welcome after becoming second-youngest player to win a world title.

stories.headline: Trump halted ‘Project Freedom’ after Saudi Arabia withheld support
stories.summary: Riyadh has now lifted suspension after call between Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and US president on Wednesday

stories.headline: High-Frequency Trader IMC Is Spending Big to Build Team in India
stories.summary: It was an unusual Sunday for 30-year-old Jake Yi, who stood with his team in the middle of a Mumbai trading floor as the screens flashed red.

stories.headline: Record Stock Rally Slips on Iran Clash, Oil Gains: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks pulled back on the final trading day of the week, while crude oil rose as escalating Middle East tensions revived concerns over energy supplies, testing the durability of a rally that had lifted equities to record.

stories.headline: Macquarie Profit Jumps, Commodities Chief Paid $25.5 Million
stories.summary: Macquarie Group Ltd.’s commodities and global markets division chief Simon Wright got a 56% pay bump after profit within the bank’s key business drove earnings growth.

stories.headline: US Yields at 5% Tug Traders Between Dip-Buying Greed and Fear
stories.summary: In some circles on Wall Street, the hottest debate isn’t about a tech-stock bubble or $100 oil as the new norm. It’s whether 30-year Treasury yields will mount a sustained push over 5%.

stories.headline: Oil Loading Logjam at Key Oman Port Puts Flows Under Strain
stories.summary: Delays to crude loading at a vital Omani terminal outside of the Strait of Hormuz last month have upended collection schedules and may lead to late deliveries to buyers already starved of Middle Eastern supply.

stories.headline: Fetterman Says He Has ‘No Plans’ to Leave the Democratic Party
stories.summary: In an opinion piece in The Washington Post, the Pennsylvania senator wrote that while he was “at odds” with the party’s approach to some issues, his values had not changed.

stories.headline: Graham Platner and Susan Collins Duel in High-Stakes Maine Senate Race Ads
stories.summary: In strikingly different ads, Graham Platner argued that “Susan Collins’s charade is over” while Senator Collins highlighted the economic benefits she has brought to Maine.

stories.headline: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
stories.summary: Jeremiah Manele toppled after months of political upheaval in the nation seen as one of China’s closest partners in the Pacific Solomon Islands prime minister Jeremiah Manele lost power in a no-confidence vote in the South Pacific country’s parliament, ending months of political uncertainty.Parliament was adjourned to allow the governor general to make arrangements for the election of a new prime minister. stories.headline: Trump says US to pause operation to guide vessels through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: "Project Freedom", which began on Monday, will be halted because progress has been made toward a deal with Iran, the US president says.

stories.headline: Russia ignores Ukraine's unilateral ceasefire and attacks kindergarten
stories.summary: Ukraine's president says Russia has "spurned the ceasefire" and Kyiv is deciding on further action to take.

stories.headline: 'Enjoy the show. Ignore the war': Venice Biennale faces backlash after including Russia
stories.summary: Protests have taken place at the prestigious arts fair over the inclusion of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Not all oil giants are prospering from the Iran war
stories.summary: Exxon and Chevron have benefited less than their European rivals

stories.headline: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since Hezbollah ceasefire
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted a senior Hezbollah figure in the capital, the first strike of its kind since mid-April.

stories.headline: China calls for Strait to be reopened 'as soon as possible' in Iran talks
stories.summary: Wang Yi is holding talks with Iran's foreign minister Aragchi who is on his first trip to China since the war began.

stories.headline: Key bridge linking North Korea and Russia almost finished, satellite images show
stories.summary: The road crossing is the latest sign of the deepening relationship between Pyongyang and Moscow.

stories.headline: What is Trump's 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The US president said the operation has been paused after starting on Monday.

stories.headline: IS families in Syria have booked tickets home to Australia, minister says
stories.summary: The four women and nine children with links to Islamic State have spent years in a Syrian camp.

stories.headline: Only one of Berkshire Hathaway and SoftBank can survive
stories.summary: The two represent competing visions of the future

stories.headline: Singapore introduces caning for boys who bully others at school
stories.summary: Under new guidelines caning will only be used in schools for male students aged nine and aboveMale school students who bully others, including through cyberbullying, will face caning as a “last resort” under new guidelines introduced in Singapore.Male students can face up to three strokes of the cane under the new rules, which were discussed in parliament on Tuesday. stories.headline: Finding soldier Tom: Solving family mystery of WW2 Soviet prisoner of war
stories.summary: He was saved by a Jersey family after escaping a Nazi labour camp, but after the war he disappeared in the Soviet Union.

stories.headline: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia
stories.summary: The explosion is the latest accident in the locality of Sutatausa, the site of another deadly mine disaster in 2023.

stories.headline: French political row over calls for overhaul and €1bn cuts at public broadcaster
stories.summary: A parliamentary committee of enquiry levelled charges of left-wing bias at state-owned France Télévisions and Radio France.

stories.headline: Deadly China plane crash was caused by fuel cut-off, says report
stories.summary: The Boeing 737 plane crashed into a Chinese hillside in 2022, killing all 132 people on board.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.
